mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS level vs 20-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and equity drawdowns, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: High retail sales (RSAFS) concurrent with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust final demand supported by full employment, a combination that amplifies earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; either condition alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and inverted UNRATE produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a two-factor regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, elevated industrial production (INDPRO) demonstrates supply-side resilience that offsets discount-rate pressure, causing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days; this relationship is absent when policy is accommodative.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, INDPRO growth fails to positively predict 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC <= 0) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month change shows Rank IC < 0.03 versus 5-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy sharply raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a Goldilocks demand environment where construction activity accelerates without triggering Fed tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction of HOUST and inverted CPIAUCSL shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal does not outperform the sum of univariate signals by at least 50% in Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strength in retail sales (RSAFS) over a quarter signals durable consumer demand that supports earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS 3-month momentum produces Rank IC < 0.03 versus 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.
